channel.title: AI & Tech News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregated AI and Tech news from 11 sources

item.title: Big Tech backs Anthropic in fight against Trump administration
item.description: [BBC Technology] A group representing tech giants called government action against Anthropic a "temper tantrum".

item.title: IAB proposes new payment rules for AI content access
item.description: [MarTech] The IAB Tech Lab’s new protocol aims to create payment rails for AI systems using publisher content, potentially reshaping how information is valued online. The post IAB proposes new payment rules for AI content access appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Why marketing needs a decision infrastructure for AI
item.description: [MarTech] AI excels where structure already exists. Context graphs could help marketing capture decision logic and make AI systems more reliable. The post Why marketing needs a decision infrastructure for AI appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: 'Icky and heartbreaking': The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom
item.description: [BBC Technology] The BBC talks to a Philippines-based woman paid to pretend to be an OnlyFans star in online chats.

item.title: Why vibe coding will disrupt martech’s buy-or-build debate
item.description: [MarTech] AI tools could let marketers fill techstack product gaps without waiting on vendors, but it also requires taking on new responsibilities. The post Why vibe coding will disrupt martech’s buy-or-build debate appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Singapore-based Stryv acquires water purifier brand Sterra
item.description: [Business Times] Roy Ang believes both companies can hit their goal of achieving an overall nine-figure revenue in 2027

item.title: Human brain cells run new data centres in Singapore, Melbourne
item.description: [Business Times] AI is driving a surge in electricity demand, forcing governments and technology companies to search for more efficient computing systems

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Anthropic’s DOD Lawsuit, War Memes, and AI Coming for VC Jobs
item.description: [WIRED AI] In today’s episode, we discuss how the saga between Anthropic and the Department of Defense is far from over.

item.title: Google Is Not Ruling Out Ads in Gemini
item.description: [WIRED AI] WIRED spoke with Nick Fox, Google’s SVP of knowledge and information, about how AI is changing the company’s advertising business.

item.title: Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
item.description: [The Verge AI] Facebook Marketplace is adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically respond to those annoying "Is this still available?" messages. You can toggle on the auto-reply option when cr

item.title: Grammarly pulls AI author-impersonation tool after backlash
item.description: [BBC Technology] Writers criticised the feature which used their names and styles as "AI personas" without consent.

item.title: Gemini’s task automation is here and it’s wild
item.description: [The Verge AI] A couple of weeks ago, Google and Samsung announced a big Gemini development coming to their newest devices: task automation. Starting with food delivery and rideshare apps, Gemini would be able to use certain apps on your behalf in a virtual window to take care of things like ordering dinner or get

item.title: Lloyds, Bank of Scotland and Halifax apps showed customers other users' transactions
item.description: [BBC Technology] The Lloyds Banking Group customers reported being able to view payments and charges from other sources.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude AI can respond with charts, diagrams, and other visuals now
item.description: [The Verge AI] Anthropic's latest update to Claude will allow the AI chatbot to generate custom charts, diagrams, and other visualizations during your conversation. If Claude determines a visual is useful based on the context of your chat, it will insert the image in-line, rather than in its side panel. As an exam

item.title: The latest AI-powered martech news and releases
item.description: [MarTech] Zendesk is betting AI agents will handle most customer service interactions, and its $200M-plus Forethought acquisition shows how serious it is. The post The latest AI-powered martech news and releases appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Anthropic doesn’t trust the Pentagon, and neither should you
item.description: [The Verge AI] Today we’re talking about the messy, fast-moving situation at Anthropic, the maker of Claude that now finds itself in a very ugly legal battle with the Pentagon.  The back-and-forth is complicated, but as of a few days ago, the Pentagon had deemed Anthropic a supply chain risk, and Anthropic ha

item.title: AI is repricing the marketing stack, not collapsing it
item.description: [MarTech] AI is making coordination tools easier to replicate, putting pricing pressure on parts of the marketing stack that deliver convenience rather than operational risk. The post AI is repricing the marketing stack, not collapsing it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Social media firms asked to toughen up age checks for under-13s
item.description: [BBC Technology] Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube and Roblox are among the platforms UK regulators say aren't putting children's safety at the heart of their products.

item.title: Oil extends gains as Middle East conflict threatens export facilities
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Oil prices surge as Kharg attack raises stakes in Middle East conflict
item.description: [Straits Times] US struck military sites on Kharg Island, which handles the bulk of Iran’s oil shipments.

item.title: ByteDance reportedly pauses global launch of its Seedance 2.0 video generator
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] The company is reportedly delaying the launch as its engineers and lawyers work to avert further legal issues.

item.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

item.title: Wiz investor unpacks Google’s $32B acquisition
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Shardul Shah of Index Ventures walks us through Google's biggest acquisition ever.

item.title: AI companies want to harvest improv actors’ skills to train AI on human emotion
item.description: [The Verge AI] If you've got strong creative instincts, the ability to authentically portray emotion, and are capable of staying true to a character's voice throughout a scene, there's a job listing calling for your experience. The catch: You won't be performing in a theater, a film studio, or an underground perfo

item.title: China AI startup Moonshot snags funds at US$18 billion valuation
item.description: [Business Times] [BEIJING] Moonshot AI is seeking to raise as much as US$1 billion in an expanded funding round that would value the startup...

item.title: Who covers AI business blunders? Some insurers cautiously step up
item.description: [Straits Times] Some insurance policies now have “absolute AI exclusion” clauses that deny coverage for AI-related mishaps.

item.title: 工业级AI视频厂商再融资，掌握120TB独家数据，营收破亿 | 硬氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 硬氪获悉，重庆达瓦合志影像科技有限公司（下称“达瓦科技”）宣布完成近亿元的新一轮融资。本轮融资由一村资本、宝捷会创新基金领投，老股东启赋资本、重庆永川国资平台持续跟投。 达瓦科技成立于2023年，总部位于重庆，是国内首个实现商业化闭环的专业级AI内容创作平台，并打通“生产+交易+履约”全链条的视频AI生态。2025年，达瓦科技营收突破亿元，三年复合增长率近300%，AI平台实现规模化收入。 达瓦科技的核心壁垒，建立在极其稀缺的120TB独家影视级结构化数据集之上。这套数据集包含镜头级、光学级、表演级等3

item.title: AWE首秀亮王牌：慕思，凭什么让一众科技大佬集体“躺平”？
item.description: [36氪] 智能家居发展十余年，各类智能单品早已走进千家万户，但一个困局却始终未能突破：单品“智能化”多流于表面，且彼此孤立，难以串联成一个真正懂生活、主动服务人真实需求的“智能之家”。 比如用于交流的语音助手，虽能随叫随应，但也会成为某一刻的噪音；又如守护安全的摄像头，并不能感知被褥之下人体的真实状态；即便是可掌控全屋智能的手机，却也不是24小时被人贴身操控。 “全域协同”、“主动服务”、“无感存在”至今仍是智能家居难以破解的重要问题。 而今，作为硬件智能家居的年度风向标，AWE2026将AI融合下的科技，投向硬件智能与家居场景的深度适配，破解“智能不智”的痛点。 所以可以看到，在这个新

item.title: Me and My Money: He started tailoring at 16 after his family lost everything
item.description: [Straits Times] Mr Suresh Mulchand also tried several other jobs before eventually returning to tailoring.

item.title: Parties fight over $500k renovation deal that was done over WhatsApp
item.description: [Straits Times] The case highlights the risk of agreeing to renovations without signing a proper agreement on the project.

item.title: US Army announces contract with Anduril worth up to $20B
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] The Army described this as a single enterprise contract consolidating more than 120 separate "procurement actions."

item.title: Musk says Tesla's mega AI chip fab project to launch in seven days
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Meta reportedly considering layoffs that could affect 20% of the company
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] These layoffs could help Facebook's parent company offset its aggressive spending on AI infrastructure, as well as AI-related acquisitions and hiring.

item.title: US Commerce Department withdraws planned rule on AI chip exports
item.description: [Business Times] [NEW YORK] The US Commerce Department withdrew a planned rule on artificial intelligence (AI) chip exports on Friday (Mar 13), the latest...

item.title: The price isn’t right: Oil spike fuels risks in South-east Asia’s tech scene
item.description: [Business Times] RISING oil prices triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East could quickly drive up operating costs within South-east Asia’s tech...

item.title: 大模型时代之后，AI正在寻找真实场景
item.description: [钛媒体] 当大模型仍在算力与参数的竞赛中不断加速时，以海信为代表的中国制造企业已经给出了另一种路径：让AI回到真实世界，在具体产品和真实场景中兑现技术价值。

item.title: 清越科技发布双品牌战略，将拓展服务机器人核心部件领域丨最前线
item.description:
[36氪] 作者丨邱晓芬 3月13日，PeroPure清越科技正式宣布旗下两大核心品牌的战略升级——专注家电领域的“配盾”品牌升级为清洁电器全生态供应商、健康家电解决方案定制商；新品牌“Show Robotics“未来将聚焦服务机器人核心功能组件。 PeroPure清越科技起源于2019年的斯坦福大学电化学实验室，在水基电化学领域积累了深厚的技术沉淀与产业经验。
清越科技 在发布会现场，PeroPure清越科技CEO徐蜂凯表示，配盾将彻底打破“单一元器件供应商”的定位，成为清洁电器全生态供应商与健康家电解决方案定制商。 据介绍，依托近百项电化学技术体系，配盾模块以自来水为原料，在

item.title: 车规级碳化硅模块厂商再融资亿元，加速入局AI算力中心基建 | 硬氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 作者 | 乔钰杰 编辑 | 袁斯来 硬氪获悉，高性能SiC（碳化硅）模块厂家无锡利普思半导体有限公司（下称“利普思”）近日完成亿元PreB+轮融资，新投资方为扬州国金、扬州龙投资本。资金将主要用于公司在扬州布局专业化车规级SiC（碳化硅）模块封装测试基地和市场推广。 利普思成立于2019年，是一家专注于第三代功率半导体SiC模块设计、生产与销售的硬科技企业。 功率半导体是控制电能转换的核心器件。与传统IGBT模块相比，SiC模块具有更高耐压、更低损耗和更高开关频率等优势，在新能源汽车主驱、超级快充、储能、电网设备以及AI算力电源等高压高功

item.title: 对话Canva可画中国王可辛：AI始终是人的乙方｜AGI独家
item.description: [钛媒体] 澳洲估值最高科技公司的成长法则。

item.title: 独家对话灵心巧手周永：他要造100万双灵巧手，只想成为机器猫的万能口袋｜AI Founder 请回答
item.description: [钛媒体] 那不是傲慢，而是他真的看见了那个世界。

item.title: Ride-hailing app inDrive says revenue up 31% in 2025, eyes delivery expansion
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: 「龙虾」炸出了腾讯的AI底牌
item.description: [钛媒体] 拥有Agent的微信可能会从人与人的连接器，真正升级到世界的连接器。

item.title: 央视315曝光AI“投毒”，我们和几位GEO从业者聊了聊
item.description: [钛媒体] 央视315晚会曝光AI大模型遭‘投毒’现象，GEO技术成为品牌在生成式AI时代争夺流量的新手段。

item.title: Taiwan likely to hold rates steady on strong economic growth: Reuters poll
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: 从泡椒凤爪到AI投毒，315撕下了多少消费企业的“新装”？
item.description: [钛媒体] 终将被审判

item.title: Micron plans second chip facility at newly acquired Taiwan site
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: 《直播电商消费者满意度调查报告（2026）》发布，消费者呼吁强化源头治理
item.description:
[36氪] 3月14日，中新经纬研究院、天津市消费者协会、北京大学电子商务法研究中心、北京阳光消费大数据研究院、消费者网联合发布《直播电商消费者满意度调查报告（2026）》。
《报告》指出，直播电商已成为主流消费渠道。在消费品类方面，美妆、食品、家居位列前三。直播电商最突出的问题中，消费者选择“商品假冒伪劣或以次充好”“价格不透明或虚假促销”“虚假宣传或夸大功效”占比分别为49.27%、39.18%、35.80%。 直播电商成为主流消费渠道
根据《报告》，当前，直播电商用户渗透率达99.63%，消费行为呈现高频、刚需特征。其中，18-45岁中青年在职群体成为主要消费人群，占比89.

item.title: 两位清华校友创业：获谷歌、英伟达系投资人押注，要把机器人数据采集成本降50倍丨36氪首发
item.description: [36氪] 作者丨欧雪 编辑丨袁斯来 硬氪获悉，面向机器人与空间智能领域的数据基础设施服务商——Ropedia完成了千万美金级种子轮融资。本轮融资由多位来自谷歌，英伟达，亚马逊的北美天使投资人和亚洲头部美元基金联合投资，深渡资本担任长期独家财务顾问。资金将主要用于核心技术团队扩建、现有产品量产交付，以及市场的持续拓展。 Ropedia于2025年下半年在新加坡成立，致力于为机器人、空间、物理智能等领域提供新一代数据采集与解决方案。 Ropedia由三位联合创始人创立：CEO陈昭熹博士（清华大学本科，南洋理工大学博士，曾在Meta参与光学动捕数据体系搭建）、CTO洪方舟博士（清华大学本科，南

item.title: China’s power ‘supergrid’ gives Xi buffer against energy shocks
item.description: [Straits Times] China has spent heavily in recent years on infrastructure to absorb more renewables and curb its reliance on imports.

item.title: Asia shares cautious, oil gains on Hormuz doubts
item.description: [CNA Tech]

item.title: Google, Accel India accelerator chooses 5 startups and none are ‘AI wrappers’
item.description: [TechCrunch AI] Google and Accel say about 70% of AI startup pitches tied to India were "wrappers" as they reviewed more than 4,000 applications for their Atoms cohort.

item.title: Honda’s car troubles began long before its disastrous bet on EVs
item.description: [Straits Times] Honda stunned investors last week by dropping a 2.5 trillion yen (S$20.1 billion) impairment charge bomb.

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
item.description: [The Verge AI] Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company wil

item.title: Most consumers use AI, but few fully trust it
item.description: [MarTech] New research shows AI is already shaping product discovery and purchases, but a large gap between usage and trust is creating new challenges for marketers. The post Most consumers use AI, but few fully trust it appeared first on MarTech.

item.title: Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
item.description: [WIRED AI] The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.

item.title: China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
item.description: [WIRED AI] Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.

item.title: Gamers’ Worst Nightmares About AI Are Coming True
item.description: [WIRED AI] From the global RAM shortage driving up console prices to job loss in the industry, gaming is shaping up to be one of the AI boom's biggest casualties.

item.title: Palantir Demos Show How the Military Could Use AI Chatbots to Generate War Plans
item.description: [WIRED AI] Software demos and Pentagon records detail how chatbots like Anthropic’s Claude could help the Pentagon analyze intelligence and suggest next steps.

item.title: AI toys for children misread emotions and respond inappropriately, researchers warn
item.description: [BBC Technology] In first study of its kind, Cambridge researchers found AI toys could misread some children's emotions.
